_meta.purpose: Source-of-truth band facts: members, contact, sound, originals, scene. Read by the website (future), referenced by AI for any band-facing copy, and migrated from the retired project-context.json.

band.name: Bolo Boys

name_rules.verb_form: Bolo-fy / Bolo-fied — used to describe the band's signature reinterpretation of a song.

service_area._purpose: Cities and regions Bolo Boys actively books in. Distinct from primary_locations (where members live). Used for SEO areaServed signal and venue outreach scoping. Update when active booking interest expands.
service_area.notes: Active booking radius is roughly metro Atlanta extending to Macon (south), Jasper (north), and Chattanooga (across the Tennessee line).

band.tagline: High-energy singalongs, throwbacks & originals — live music built to bring people together.

members.name: Paul
members.background: Childhood friend of Nigel; consulting background brings structure to the band's operations.
members.songwriting: Three originals: a punk-inspired Atlanta song, a slower country/bluegrass piece featuring solos from Nigel and John, and a Western song about a man on the open road.

members.name: Nigel
members.background: Lifelong musician; Paul's childhood friend from North Georgia. Moved to the West Coast and reconnected with Paul and John when he returned to Macon in 2024 — the moment Bolo Boys started.
members.songwriting: Has an original Western song released on Spotify; more Bolo Boys recordings in progress.

members.name: John
members.background: Paul's mom's cousin and family. Has known Nigel since Nigel was born. Retired engineer and a strong bluegrass guitarist — brings classics like Doc Watson's Tennessee Stud into the set, opening the band to whole new demographics.

contact.calendar_policy: Shared band calendar — every event invite goes to all three members.

sound.signature_style: Bolo-fied — a song gets played until it has the band's spin on it. The barometer of success is when wildly different covers feel like they're from the same album. Southern twang welcome — it's authentic, not a costume.
sound.originals_philosophy: Originals are woven into the set so seamlessly that listeners don't notice — being able to nestle them between Petty and Marley without losing the room is the success metric. Total of four originals in active rotation.
sound.mission_line: You'll wind up dancing and singing along — reliving songs from the back of your parents' car, and meeting some new ones.

live_show.typical_set: ~2 hours, two sets with a ~20-minute break
live_show.pa: Fully self-contained — band brings, sets up, and runs their own sound.
live_show.ethos: Take it seriously enough to be professional and earn each venue's trust. Build the next show by making this one count.
live_show.minimum_set_length: Will not book sets shorter than 30 minutes — setup and prep don't justify it, and shorter sets don't let the band drive the room.

scene.scene_notes: Friends and frequent co-billers; all lean Western/Appalachian. Bolo Boys learn from them and have been influenced by them in turn.

what_we_are_not: Not THE Bolo Boys — just Bolo Boys.
Not a wedding band — fun and singalong-friendly, but not the corporate-event format.
Not a tribute act — covers get Bolo-fied, not reproduced.

social.spotify_artist_note: Nigel has a Western original released on Spotify; a Bolo Boys artist presence is being built out.

scene_collaborators.name: Ante Up
scene_collaborators.note: Supergroup composed of members from Bolo Boys, Dirty Shame, and Acoustic Station. No separate social presence — render as plain text.

originals.title: The Cowboy's Life
originals.performer_credit: Nigel's solo recording. Bolo Boys play it live.

guest_co_bills.name: Adam Klein
guest_co_bills.note: Atlanta singer-songwriter. One-time co-bill (Grant Ole Opry, 2026-05-25). Linked on the site via supporting_acts, but not a recurring scene relationship — kept separate from scene_collaborators.

trajectory.current_focus: Getting better with every show; building venue relationships in Atlanta; weaving more originals into sets.
trajectory.twelve_month_picture: Packed outdoor courtyards. More outdoor bars and restaurants. Crowds singing the hooks back. More songs on Spotify.
